conjecture_title: Informal question on Behrend-style sets avoiding affine patterns with mixed-sign coefficients
summary: The question asks whether there exist subsets of $[N]$ with $N^{1-o(1)}$ elements avoiding linear patterns with two positive and two negative coefficients (e.g., $2x+2y=3w+z$), in the spirit of Behrend's construction. No follow-up paper resolving this question was found in a targeted web search covering the period 2019–2026. The question is related to known limitations of the Ruzsa–Bergelson–Host–Kra popular-difference approach, and Asaf Shapira's earlier work on Behrend-type constructions for linear equations treats adjacent problems, but the specific mixed-sign regime identified in the Fox–Sah–Sawhney–Stoner–Zhao paper appears to remain open. Given that the question is now roughly 7 years old, the absence of indexed results warrants low confidence.
search_queries: Behrend construction mixed sign coefficients linear equations avoiding 2x+2y=3w+z additive combinatorics
Fox Sah Sawhney Stoner Zhao triforce corners 1903.04863 Behrend popular differences
Behrend type sets avoiding two positive two negative coefficients linear equations density N^{1-o(1)} additive combinatorics 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: No follow-up resolving this question was found after 5 web calls. The closest related work is Shapira's 'Behrend-Type Constructions for Sets of Linear Equations', which predates the Fox et al. paper and treats the problem for different coefficient regimes. Recent improvements to Behrend's construction (arXiv:2401.16106, arXiv:2406.12290) concern 3-AP-free sets and do not address the mixed-sign setting. The question remains open with low confidence due to the 7-year age of the conjecture.
